name: proton-bridge-mcp
description: Access your ProtonMail via MCP — bridges Claude to Proton Bridge IMAP
long_description:
A local MCP server that bridges ProtonMail to AI agents via the Proton Mail Bridge IMAP daemon. Provides 19 tools for reading, searching, organizing, and labeling email — with operation reversal, batch operations, and audit logging.

**Key features:**
- Read tools: list folders/labels, fetch messages, search
- Mutating tools: create/delete folders & labels, mark read/unread, add/remove labels
- Reversible operations via `revert_operations`
- Per-tool annotations (`readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `openWorldHint`)
- IMAP connection pooling and audit logging

Requires a running Proton Mail Bridge instance on the local machine.

tools.name: get_folders
tools.description: List all mail folders with metadata

tools.name: list_mailbox
tools.description: Browse emails in a mailbox with pagination

tools.name: fetch_summaries
tools.description: Fetch envelope data for known email IDs

tools.name: fetch_message
tools.description: Fetch full message body and attachment metadata

tools.name: fetch_attachment
tools.description: Download a single attachment by part ID

tools.name: search_mailbox
tools.description: Full-text IMAP search within a mailbox

tools.name: create_folder
tools.description: Create a new mail folder under Folders/

tools.name: create_label
tools.description: Create a new Proton Mail label

tools.name: delete_folder
tools.description: Delete a mail folder (irreversible — clears operation history)

tools.name: delete_label
tools.description: Delete a Proton Mail label (irreversible — clears operation history)

tools.name: move_emails
tools.description: Move a batch of emails to another mailbox

tools.name: mark_read
tools.description: Mark a batch of emails as read

tools.name: mark_unread
tools.description: Mark a batch of emails as unread

tools.name: add_labels
tools.description: Add Proton Mail labels to a batch of emails

tools.name: remove_labels
tools.description: Remove Proton Mail labels from a batch of emails

tools.name: revert_operations
tools.description: Reverse a range of tracked operations

prompts.name: summarize_inbox
prompts.description: Summarize unread emails in your INBOX
prompts.text: List the ${arguments.count} most recent unread emails in my INBOX. For each, give me a one-line summary including sender and subject.

prompts.name: find_emails_from
prompts.description: Find emails from a specific sender
prompts.text: Search my inbox for all emails from ${arguments.sender} in the last 30 days. Show me the subjects and dates.

prompts.name: label_emails
prompts.description: Apply a label to emails matching a query
prompts.text: Find all emails matching '${arguments.query}' and apply the label '${arguments.label}'. Show me what you did and give me the operation ID so I can revert if needed.

prompts.name: cleanup_folder
prompts.description: Review and clean up a folder
prompts.text: List the emails in '${arguments.folder}'. Identify any that look like they could be deleted (newsletters, expired notifications, automated alerts). Don't delete anything yet — show me your recommendations first.

prompts.name: organize_inbox
prompts.description: Suggest labels and folder moves to organize your inbox
prompts.text: Look at the ${arguments.count} most recent emails in my INBOX. Group them by topic or sender, and suggest a label or folder for each group. Don't apply anything yet — just show me your proposed organization plan.

bridge_password.title: Bridge Mailbox Password
bridge_password.description: Bridge-generated mailbox password (from Proton Bridge app → Account → Mailbox password). NOT your ProtonMail login password.

bridge_host.title: Bridge IMAP Host
bridge_host.description: Hostname of the Proton Bridge IMAP server

bridge_imap_port.title: Bridge IMAP Port
bridge_imap_port.description: Port of the Proton Bridge IMAP server

pool_min.title: Connection Pool Min
pool_min.description: Minimum number of IMAP connections to keep open

pool_max.title: Connection Pool Max
pool_max.description: Maximum number of concurrent IMAP connections

pool_idle_drain_secs.title: Idle Drain (seconds)
pool_idle_drain_secs.description: Drain pool to minimum after this many idle seconds

pool_idle_timeout_secs.title: Idle Timeout (seconds)
pool_idle_timeout_secs.description: Empty pool entirely after this many idle seconds (0 = disabled)

log_level.title: Log Level
log_level.description: Logging verbosity: trace | debug | info | warn | error
